goal: Match the render to a brand plate: rotate HDRI for key direction, dial tonemap/exposure/contrast to match.
thoughts: HDRI rotation via its rotation attribute is faster than reloading the skydome. Tonemap/exposure/bloom/vignette stages named explicitly -- never 'make it look better'. If compositor has Resolve/Nuke downstream, render linear EXR and grade there rather than baking grade into beauty. A/B against the plate via capture_viewport.
code:
create_hdri_skydome(
    path="/World/Lighting/SkyDome",
    texture_path="<HDRI_EXR>",
    intensity="<FLOAT>",
    rotation="<DEGREES_TO_MATCH_KEY>"
)
# or rotate existing dome:
set_attribute(
    prim_path="/World/Lighting/SkyDome",
    attribute_name="xformOp:rotateY",
    value="<DEGREES>"
)
enable_post_process(
    tonemap="ACES",
    exposure="<EV>",
    contrast="<FLOAT>",
    bloom_enabled=True,
    bloom_intensity="<FLOAT>",
    vignette_enabled=False
)
capture_viewport(path="<AB_PNG>", width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Color-grading in the renderer when Resolve/Nuke is downstream
Vague 'tweak tonemap' without naming exposure/contrast/bloom explicitly
Rotating the dome but not confirming which axis matches Z-up Isaac Sim
